senses.definition: to attract or gain interest, approval, or admiration

examples.x: His speech appealed to voters' desire for change and better opportunities.
The art exhibition appealed to art enthusiasts with its diverse range of styles and themes.
The vacation destination appealed to travelers with its picturesque scenery and cultural attractions.
The restaurant's menu appealed to foodies with its innovative dishes and use of local ingredients.
The music festival appealed to music lovers with its lineup of popular artists and variety of genres.
The novel's unique storyline and compelling characters appealed to readers of all ages.

senses.definition: to seek information or advice from someone, especially before making a decision or doing something

examples.x: The lawyer consulted with her colleagues to prepare for the upcoming trial.
Before purchasing the new equipment, I consulted the user reviews online to see if it was worth it.
I consulted with my friends before making a decision about which college to attend.
Before accepting the job offer, I consulted with my family to get their input.
Before starting the project, we should consult the project manager to clarify any uncertainties.

senses.definition: to formally oppose or challenge a decision or a statement

examples.x: She plans to contest the election results in court.
The athlete decided to contest the referee's decision during the game.
They will contest the new policy at the upcoming board meeting.
He intends to contest the charges brought against him in court.
They filed paperwork to contest the patent granted to their competitor.

senses.definition: to make someone feel upset, shocked, and sometimes offended about something

examples.x: His rude behavior towards the waiter disgusted everyone at the table.
Her insensitive remarks about the tragedy disgusted the survivors.
The politician's corrupt actions disgusted the citizens.
Seeing the litter strewn across the beautiful park disgusted the environmentalists.
The offensive language used by the comedian disgusted many audience members.

senses.definition: to prefer someone or something to an alternative

examples.x: I favor spending my weekends in nature rather than in the city.
She favors the blue dress over the red one for the party.
He tends to favor action movies over romantic comedies.
The manager may favor candidates with relevant experience during the hiring process.
We favor a collaborative approach to problem-solving in our team.

senses.definition: someone or something that one likes more among others of the same kind

examples.x: Among all the books on the shelf, this novel is my absolute favorite.
In the world of sports, soccer is often cited as a favorite among many enthusiasts.

senses.definition: to choose something among other things

examples.x: She couldn't decide between the two dresses, so she went for the one with the floral pattern.
At the restaurant, he decided to go for the seafood pasta.
He knew he had to go for the best solution among the available options.
When shopping for a new phone, she decided to go for the latest model.
I'll go for the salmon from the menu; it's my favorite dish.

senses.definition: to avoid an activity, person, etc. because one is scared, unwilling, or not confident

examples.x: He tends to shy away from public speaking due to his nervousness.
The company decided to shy away from risky investments after the last financial crisis.
She shies away from conflict and prefers to maintain harmony in the workplace.

senses.definition: to come to a final decision or conclusion after considering different options or possibilities

examples.x: After much consideration, she finally made up her mind to pursue her dream of starting her own business.
He couldn't make up his mind between the two job offers, as both had their own unique advantages.
Despite the conflicting opinions from friends, she made up her mind to continue her studies abroad.
After trying different flavors, he made up his mind and chose the chocolate chip ice cream as his favorite.

senses.definition: to choose among a group of people or things

examples.x: Out of all the puppies, he picked out the one with the white patch on its forehead.
She took her time to pick out the perfect dress for the event.
They asked the children to pick out their favorite toys.

senses.definition: to tolerate something or someone unpleasant, often without complaining

examples.x: Parents often put up with the messiness of young children for the joy they bring.
She puts up with the challenges of her demanding job for the sake of career growth.
Friends put up with each other's quirks and differences to maintain strong relationships.
Residents put up with the noise of city life for the conveniences it offers.
Teachers put up with the complexities of virtual classrooms to ensure students' education.

senses.definition: to consider a matter carefully before reaching a decision

examples.x: She needs time to think over whether to accept the invitation.
Before buying a house, it's wise to think over your financial situation.
We should think over the consequences of our actions.

senses.definition: to consider all the known facts and details before making a final decision

examples.x: The design takes account of environmental concerns.
It is important to take account of different perspectives in a discussion.
When planning the event, they need to take account of the weather forecast.
They must take account of cultural differences when designing the program.
The manager will take account of employee feedback before making changes.

senses.definition: to seek guidance, help, or advice from someone

examples.x: She turned to her friend for advice on the difficult decision.
In times of uncertainty, communities often turn to leaders for direction.
During the health scare, individuals turned to healthcare professionals for guidance.
When facing a problem, it's important to turn to family for assistance.
She turned to her mentor for advice on starting her own business.
During difficult times, people often turn to their friends for emotional support.

senses.definition: used to express a preference for one option over another

examples.x: She would rather stay home and read a book than go out to a party.
He would rather take the train than drive in heavy traffic.
They would rather save money for a vacation than spend it on expensive dinners.
I would rather wait for the next bus than take a crowded one.

senses.definition: a person or thing responsible for making important choices or judgments, especially within an organization

examples.x: As the CEO, she is the ultimate decision maker for the company's strategic direction.
The committee members are decision makers in selecting the winner of the scholarship.
In healthcare, doctors are decision makers regarding patient treatment plans.
The board of directors serves as decision makers for approving major investments.
As a parent, you are often the decision maker for your children's upbringing and education.

senses.definition: the feeling of not liking something or someone

examples.x: She has a strong dislike for spicy foods.
His dislike of loud music made the concert unpleasant for him.
There is a general dislike for rainy weather among the children.
There is a growing dislike for pollution in the community.

senses.definition: a strong liking for one option or choice over another based on personal taste, favor, etc.

examples.x: His preference for spicy food means he often orders dishes with extra chili.
She has a strong preference for classical music over contemporary genres.
The company surveyed employees to understand their preferences for workplace amenities.
Our preferences in movies often reflect our individual interests and values.
The candidate's policy proposals align closely with the preferences of young voters.

senses.definition: a firm decision to do something or to behave in a certain way, often made after careful consideration

examples.x: She made a resolution to finish writing her novel by the end of the year.
Setting a resolution to save more money each month helped him achieve his financial goals.
She made a New Year's resolution to exercise more regularly.
His resolution to quit smoking was motivated by concerns for his health.
He stuck to his resolution of reading one book per month.

senses.definition: the ability to recognize something with good quality or high standard, especially in art, style, beauty, etc., based on personal preferences

examples.x: Her taste in clothing reflects her keen eye for elegant and timeless fashion.
He has a discerning taste in art, preferring minimalist pieces with subtle complexities.
The boutique caters to customers with refined tastes in luxury accessories and apparel.
Their impeccable taste in home décor is evident in the harmonious blend of colors and textures.
Developing a sophisticated taste in fashion often involves exploring different styles and understanding personal preferences.

senses.definition: the particular characteristics that are considered when evaluating something

examples.x: The criteria for choosing the winner of the art contest are creativity and technique.
Her criteria for a good vacation include good weather and interesting activities.
The committee set several criteria for evaluating the effectiveness of the new policy.
The hiring criteria include relevant experience, education, and a positive attitude.
The criteria for this research study include patient age and medical history.

senses.definition: a positive feeling or preference toward someone or something, based on personal enjoyment, attraction, or approval

examples.x: I have a liking for spicy food because it excites my taste buds.
Her liking for adventure novels led her to collect books by various authors.
His liking for classical music developed from listening to it with his grandparents.
The children showed a liking for the new teacher because she made learning fun.
My liking for gardening grew after spending time with my grandmother in her garden.

senses.definition: the likeliness to become or do something

examples.x: Her tendency to procrastinate often leads to last-minute stress.
Her tendency to interrupt others made conversations difficult.
He showed a tendency to favor one side in the debate.
His tendency to be late caused some frustration among his friends.
There is a growing tendency toward remote work among companies.

senses.definition: as a second choice or another possibility

examples.x: If you prefer not to drive, you can alternatively take the train to the city.
Instead of using traditional flour, you can alternatively use almond flour in the recipe.
The meeting can be scheduled for Monday, or alternatively, on Wednesday to accommodate everyone's availability.
You can purchase the product online, or alternatively, visit the store in person.
If the weather is unfavorable for outdoor activities, you can alternatively explore indoor entertainment options.

senses.definition: to make someone satisfied or happy

examples.x: The musician pleases the crowd by playing her favorite song.
The manager went out of her way to please the demanding customer.
The artist painted a beautiful portrait to please her client.
The teacher adjusted her teaching style to please her students and keep them engaged.
He pleases his parents by cleaning up the house before they return from their trip.

senses.definition: as a replacement or equal in value, amount, etc.

examples.x: I was going to go out for dinner, but I decided to cook at home instead.
We had to cancel our trip due to the weather, so we watched a movie instead.
I didn’t have any milk for my coffee, so I used cream instead.
She decided to take the bus instead.

senses.definition: to behave or speak in a way that is offensive to someone or something

examples.x: She disrespected her teacher by talking back in class.
The athlete disrespected his opponent by taunting them.
He was warned about disrespecting the authority figures at school.
She had disrespected the traditions during the ceremony.

senses.definition: to have a strong longing or intense desire for something or someone

examples.x: I'm dying for a cup of coffee right now.
He's dying to see the new movie in theaters.
She's dying to try that new restaurant in town.
They're dying to know the secret you've been keeping.
We're dying to hear about your latest adventure.
He's dying to show off his new guitar skills.
